title: Relabel vol-target to the truth (decouple from leverage cap); guarded cap-raise deferred
source: quant-phd-advisor re-review (2026-05-30) under the long-horizon + future-equity frame, verified against a reproduced full-history backtest (scripts/backtest_survival_book.py, 2000-01-03 -> 2026-05-29, 26.4y).
why: PREMISE FALSIFIED by risk-officer on the live regime. The '4.62% unscaled ex-ante -> need k~=1.30' arithmetic is a PRE-CRYPTO FX-only-era artifact: the 26.4y path is ~21y of 3 FX pairs alone (metals/BTC/ETH start 2021-05, SOL 2025-01). On the crypto-era window the book actually runs in, raising max_leverage 0.5->1.0 does NOT reach 6% vol; it TRIPS the -10% manual-reset latch (DD-stops 0->1344), holds the book flat, and flips Sharpe +0.51 -> -0.47. Full history at 1.3x reaches only 3.13%. So the chronic under-risking is very likely CORRECT for a no-alpha preservation mandate; the only genuine defect is the MISLEADING 6% LABEL advertising a policy the book cannot run. PRIMARY fix = relabel the target to the truth (~realized 2-3%). Cap-raise is a guarded, deferred fallback requiring a CEO mandate decision. CRITICAL omission found: there are TWO leverage controls -- max_leverage (vol-target clamp, survival_book.py:832) AND max_gross_leverage (pre-trade order gate, live_loop.py:278, the B0090 $1.19B blow-up backstop, intentionally matched). Touching one without the other either no-ops live (parity break) or re-widens the blow-up backstop.
value_rationale: H: highest-value, equity-agnostic, always-on change. Directly governs how much risk the long-horizon product actually takes; everything else is second-order while the cap binds 100%.
scope: PRIMARY: relabel target_vol to the realized truth (~2-3%) so config advertises the policy the book actually runs; no behavior change, just honest labeling.

Add a vol-estimate FLOOR to vol_target_scale (survival_book.py:222): k=target/book_vol is unbounded as book_vol->0 -- a vol collapse produces an arbitrarily large k clamped only by the cap. Required regardless of any cap decision.
DEFERRED guarded cap-raise (only if CEO mandate chooses higher risk): max_leverage<=1.0, RAISE max_gross_leverage in the SAME change, staged ramp + re-review gate.
Add a parity test asserting max_leverage == max_gross_leverage (or an explicit, tested rationale for any divergence).
Any cap change hard-gated on B0112 (evaluate on distributions, not the FX-dominated path).
recommendation: Do the relabel + vol-estimate floor FIRST (safe, no exposure increase). BLOCK any cap-raise until B0112 lands AND the CEO makes an explicit mandate decision on the target risk level. Never raise max_leverage without raising max_gross_leverage.
out_of_scope: Adding alpha/direction. This is sizing policy only.

history.reason: incorporate risk-officer falsification + max_gross_leverage coupling
history.from: Reproduced 26.4y backtest: realized ann. vol 1.52% vs 6.00% target (ratio 0.25); leverage-cap bind ratio 100%; unscaled ex-ante vol ~4.62% is itself BELOW the 6% target, so hitting target needs k~=1.30x but the 0.5x cap clamps k to 0.5, de-levering to ~2.31% ex-ante. The 6% target is UNREACHABLE BY CONSTRUCTION: the cap sits below the leverage the target requires, so the book's actual risk policy ('0.5x of an inverse-vol book') is an emergent artifact NOBODY chose. Harvey et al. 2018 + institutional risk-parity benchmarks (Bridgewater All Weather, HFR Risk Parity ~10%) put even 6% on the conservative end; realizing 1.5-2.9% forgoes years of compounding for a no-alpha capital-preservation mandate whose tail is already controlled by kills + var-caps. This is a mis-specified control, not conservatism. It is also the UNLOCK for B0110/B0111, which are near no-ops while the cap binds.
history.to: PREMISE FALSIFIED by risk-officer on the live regime. The '4.62% unscaled ex-ante -> need k~=1.30' arithmetic is a PRE-CRYPTO FX-only-era artifact: the 26.4y path is ~21y of 3 FX pairs alone (metals/BTC/ETH start 2021-05, SOL 2025-01). On the crypto-era window the book actually runs in, raising max_leverage 0.5->1.0 does NOT reach 6% vol; it TRIPS the -10% manual-reset latch (DD-stops 0->1344), holds the book flat, and flips Sharpe +0.51 -> -0.47. Full history at 1.3x reaches only 3.13%. So the chronic under-risking is very likely CORRECT for a no-alpha preservation mandate; the only genuine defect is the MISLEADING 6% LABEL advertising a policy the book cannot run. PRIMARY fix = relabel the target to the truth (~realized 2-3%). Cap-raise is a guarded, deferred fallback requiring a CEO mandate decision. CRITICAL omission found: there are TWO leverage controls -- max_leverage (vol-target clamp, survival_book.py:832) AND max_gross_leverage (pre-trade order gate, live_loop.py:278, the B0090 $1.19B blow-up backstop, intentionally matched). Touching one without the other either no-ops live (parity break) or re-widens the blow-up backstop.

history.reason: risk-officer: relabel-first, vol floor, max_gross_leverage, parity test, B0112 gate
from: Treat the vol TARGET as the policy variable and the leverage cap as a tail/low-vol safety rail that should bind rarely, not 100% of rebalances.
Decide the mandate target consciously (defensible band ~3-10%); if 1.5-2.9% is intended, RELABEL the 6% target to the true number rather than advertising a policy the book does not run.
Raise max_leverage so the chosen target drives day-to-day allocation (cap bites only when realized vol collapses, the classic vol-target failure mode).
Re-run backtest across target/cap pairs and report realized vol, maxDD, ENB, crypto var share to support the mandate decision.
Preserve the live/backtest parity invariant (single compute_survival_target).
to: PRIMARY: relabel target_vol to the realized truth (~2-3%) so config advertises the policy the book actually runs; no behavior change, just honest labeling.

Add a parity test asserting max_leverage == max_gross_leverage (or an explicit, tested rationale for any divergence).
Any cap change hard-gated on B0112 (evaluate on distributions, not the FX-dominated path).
